Home.engineTitle: A Complete Marketing Engine Inside Your Agent
Home.engineBody: Diagnostics, recommended fixes, writes, and undo - all driven by one MCP connection per ad account.
Home.affiliateBody: Refer a customer to NotFair. Get half of everything they pay us for their first 12 months. Monthly payouts, no caps.

faq.q: Will NotFair make changes without my approval?
faq.a: No. NotFair can read your account freely, but every write tool surfaces a diff first. You confirm before anything hits Google Ads - and any change is reversible with a single tool call.

faq.q: Which AI clients does this work with?
faq.a: Anything that speaks the MCP Streamable HTTP transport: Claude.ai (Web, Desktop, Cowork), Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor, Cline, OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and custom MCP clients. The server URL is the same - only the per-client config differs.

faq.q: Do I need to know what to fix before I start?
faq.a: No. Lead with the business problem - leads got expensive, ROAS slipped, something changed. The agent diagnoses the likely cause, ranks fixes by spend at risk, and drafts the campaign edits for your approval.

faq.q: What can the agent actually change?
faq.a: Keywords, negatives, bids, budgets, ads, ad groups, campaign settings, audiences, scripts, and reporting workflows - all on Google Ads. Every write is approval-gated and logged.

OAuth 2.0 with PKCE by default - Claude.ai and Codex run it automatically. For clients that don't support OAuth, generate a Bearer token at notfair.co/connect. Either way, NotFair never asks an LLM to handle credentials directly.

Yes. Connecting and running audits is free, no credit card. The Free plan includes 7 days of unlimited access, then 300 MCP operations per month forever. Upgrade to Growth when Claude becomes your daily ads operator.

faq.title: FAQ — NotFair MCP
faq.intro: Common questions about authenticating, configuring, and operating the NotFair MCP servers.

items.question: What is NotFair MCP?
items.answer: Two hosted Model Context Protocol servers — one for Google Ads, one for Meta Ads — that expose your accounts to MCP-compatible AI clients. Reads stream live campaign data; writes are proposed in chat and require explicit approval before they hit the ad platform.

items.question: Which AI clients can I use?
items.answer: Anything that speaks the MCP Streamable HTTP transport: Claude.ai (Web, Desktop, Cowork), Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor, Cline, and custom MCP clients. The server URL stays the same — only the client-side config differs.

items.question: Do I need to self-host anything?
items.answer: No. Both servers are hosted by NotFair at {googleServerUrl} and {metaServerUrl}. You connect the underlying ad account once via OAuth and point your client at the URL.

items.question: How does authentication work?
items.answer: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE is the default — Claude.ai and Codex run it automatically. For clients that don't support OAuth, you can use a Bearer token via the Authorization header. Generate either at notfair.co/connect or notfair.co/connect/meta-ads.

items.question: Can the AI write to my ad accounts?
items.answer: Only with explicit approval. Write tools propose changes, the client surfaces the diff, and you confirm before anything hits the Google Ads or Meta Marketing API. Read access is unrestricted; every write is gated.

items.question: What does it cost?
items.answer: Connecting and running audits is free with no credit card. Paid plans unlock higher usage limits and team features.

items.question: Where do I find platform-specific configs?
items.answer: The /google-ads-mcp and /meta-ads-mcp pages have generic JSON snippets that work in any MCP client. The per-client setup guides walk through Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Codex specifically.

items.question: What is the NotFair Google Ads MCP server?
items.answer: It's a hosted Model Context Protocol server that exposes your Google Ads account to MCP-compatible AI clients. Read tools provide live campaign context for diagnosis; write tools propose fixes that you approve in chat.

items.question: Which MCP clients are supported?
items.answer: Any client that speaks the MCP Streamable HTTP transport — Claude.ai (Web, Desktop, Cowork), Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor, Cline, and custom MCP clients. The server URL is the same; only the client-side config differs.

items.question: Do I need to self-host anything?
items.answer: No. The server is hosted at {serverUrl}. You just point your client at it and authenticate.

items.question: How does authentication work?
items.answer: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE is the recommended flow — Claude.ai and Codex run it automatically. For clients that don't support OAuth, you can use a Bearer token via the Authorization header. Generate either at notfair.co/connect.

items.question: Is the MCP server free?
items.answer: Yes. Connecting and running a free Google Ads audit is free with no credit card. Paid plans unlock higher usage limits and team features.

items.question: Can the AI write to my account through this server?
items.answer: Only with your explicit approval. Write tools propose changes, the client surfaces them, and you confirm before anything hits the Google Ads API. Read access is unrestricted; write access is gated.

faq.title: FAQ — NotFair Meta MCP server
faq.intro: Common questions about authenticating, configuring, and using the NotFair Meta Ads MCP server.

items.question: What is the NotFair Meta Ads MCP server?
items.answer: It's a hosted Model Context Protocol server that exposes your Meta ad accounts (Facebook + Instagram) to MCP-compatible AI clients. Read tools provide live campaign context for diagnosis; write tools propose fixes that you approve in chat.

items.question: Which MCP clients are supported?
items.answer: Any client that speaks the MCP Streamable HTTP transport — Claude.ai (Web, Desktop, Cowork), Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Cursor, Cline, and custom MCP clients. The server URL is the same; only the client-side config differs.

items.question: Do I need to self-host anything?
items.answer: No. The Meta Ads MCP server is hosted at {serverUrl}. You just point your client at it and authenticate.

items.question: How does authentication work?
items.answer: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE is the recommended flow — Claude.ai and Codex run it automatically. For clients that don't support OAuth, you can use a Bearer token via the Authorization header. Generate either at notfair.co/connect/meta-ads.

items.question: Is the MCP server free?
items.answer: Yes. Connecting to NotFair's Meta MCP is free with no credit card. Paid plans unlock higher usage limits and team features.

items.question: Can the AI write to my Meta ad accounts through this server?
items.answer: Only with your explicit approval. Write tools propose changes (pause campaigns, adjust budgets, rename entities, etc.), the client surfaces them, and you confirm before anything hits the Meta Marketing API. Read access is unrestricted; write access is gated.

items.question: Does this work for both Facebook and Instagram ads?
items.answer: Yes. Meta Ads covers both Facebook and Instagram inventory under a single ad account. The MCP exposes everything Meta surfaces through the Marketing API: campaigns, ad sets, ads, creative, audiences, insights, and the parent Business Manager.

capabilities.title: Claude AI for Google Ads — what it actually does.
capabilities.body: Once connected, Claude has live access to your account. No more exporting CSVs, copying tables into chat, or working from screenshots. Ask a question, get a specific answer backed by real data.

items.title: Campaign diagnosis in natural language
items.body: Ask Claude what is wrong. It checks campaign structure, keyword health, search term quality, impression share, and ad copy — then gives you a prioritized fix list.

items.title: Find the issues you did not know to ask for
items.body: In testing, mature accounts often have clear problems hiding in search terms, structure, match types, and budget allocation. Asking "what should I fix?" surfaces them immediately.

items.title: Every change is reviewable
items.body: NotFair never commits a change without your explicit approval. Claude shows you what it wants to do, you say yes or no. Human always in control.

items.title: Works where you already work
items.body: Claude Desktop for solo marketers. Claude Code for developers managing campaigns. Claude Cowork for teams. One MCP config, all three clients.

faq.title: FAQ — Google Ads and Claude
faq.intro: Common questions from MCP builders and marketers setting up the Claude AI Google Ads workflow.

items.question: How do I use Claude for Google Ads with NotFair?
items.answer: Add NotFair to your MCP config (one JSON snippet), connect your Google Ads account via OAuth at notfair.co, and Claude immediately gains live access to your campaigns. You can then ask Claude what is wrong, why performance changed, what to fix next, or to draft approved bid, keyword, negative, and ad changes — all in natural conversation.

items.question: Does NotFair work with Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork?
items.answer: Yes. NotFair is a standard MCP server, so it works with any MCP-compatible Claude client. Add the same config snippet to Claude Desktop settings, your Claude Code MCP config, or Claude Cowork — it works identically in all three.

items.question: What Google Ads data can Claude see through NotFair?
items.answer: Claude gets live access to campaign performance, keyword bids and Quality Scores, search term reports, ad copy, spend data, impression share, and conversion tracking status. It reads your actual account data in real time — no exports or manual uploads needed.

items.question: Can Claude make changes to my Google Ads account?
items.answer: Yes, but only with your explicit approval at each step. Claude can propose bid changes, pause campaigns, add negative keywords, and write new ads — but every action is shown to you before it executes. You review and confirm. NotFair also logs every change so you can track impact.

items.question: Is this an official Google or Anthropic integration?
items.answer: NotFair is an independent product built on Anthropic's open Model Context Protocol (MCP) standard and the Google Ads API. It is not an official Google product. MCP is the open standard Anthropic created for connecting AI to external tools — any developer can build MCP servers, and NotFair is one focused entirely on Google Ads.

items.question: What does setup actually take?
items.answer: Under 2 minutes. Paste one JSON snippet into your MCP config, open notfair.co, click Connect Google Ads, complete the OAuth flow. That's it — Claude can now access your campaigns.

items.question: Do I need to know how to code to use this?
items.answer: No coding required. Editing a JSON config file is the most technical step — it's copying and pasting one snippet. The rest is standard Google OAuth and talking to Claude in plain English.

hero.body: NotFair is a Google Ads MCP server for OpenAI's Codex CLI. One terminal command and Codex gets live access to your campaigns, keywords, spend, and ad copy — diagnose, optimize, and manage from the terminal.
hero.note: No credit card required. Human approval on every write.

capabilities.title: Codex AI for Google Ads — what it actually does.
capabilities.body: Once connected, Codex has live access to your account from the terminal. No more exporting CSVs, no copy-pasting into chat. Ask a question, get a specific answer backed by real data.

items.title: Audit from your terminal
items.body: Ask Codex what is wrong. It checks campaign structure, keyword health, search term quality, and ad copy — then gives you a prioritized fix list, right in your shell.

items.title: Pipe into your dev workflow
items.body: Codex is terminal-native. Chain Google Ads diagnostics with your scripts, CI checks, or deploy hooks. The same MCP tools work in interactive and headless modes.

items.title: Approve every write
items.body: NotFair never commits a change without your explicit approval. Codex shows you exactly what it wants to do — pause a campaign, adjust a bid, add a negative — you say yes or no.

items.title: Works on macOS, Linux, WSL
items.body: The Codex CLI runs wherever your terminal runs. macOS, Linux, and Windows via WSL are all supported out of the box with the same one-liner install.

tools.title: What Codex can read and change.
tools.body: NotFair exposes a focused set of MCP tools. Read tools give Codex live account context. Write tools let Codex propose changes — each requiring your explicit approval before execution.

items.name: listCampaigns
items.description: Pull live campaign performance, spend, and status

items.name: getKeywords
items.description: Inspect keyword bids, Quality Scores, and impressions

items.name: getSearchTermReport
items.description: Find irrelevant queries, weak match types, and budget leaks

items.name: getCampaignPerformance
items.description: Deep-dive into campaign metrics over any date range

items.name: updateBid
items.description: Adjust a keyword bid — reviewable before it applies

items.name: pauseCampaign
items.description: Pause an underperforming campaign through Codex

items.name: addNegativeKeyword
items.description: Block a wasted search term at the campaign or account level

items.name: createAd
items.description: Write and launch new ad copy through natural conversation

auditCta.title: Connect once. Let Codex find the fixes.
auditCta.body: Connect your Google Ads account and Codex can diagnose wasted spend, missed opportunities, and structural issues — then draft the changes for your approval.

faq.title: FAQ — Google Ads and OpenAI Codex
faq.intro: Common questions from developers and marketers setting up the Codex CLI Google Ads workflow.

items.question: What is the NotFair Codex integration?
items.answer: It's an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that gives OpenAI's Codex CLI live access to your Google Ads account. One terminal command wires it up — Codex can then diagnose issues, recommend fixes, and propose changes you approve in chat.

items.question: How is this different from the Claude integration?
items.answer: Same backend, different client. The Codex CLI calls the NotFair MCP server using OpenAI’s MCP support. The Claude Connector and Claude Code plugin call the same server from Anthropic’s clients. Capabilities are identical — only the client differs.

items.question: What kinds of Google Ads tasks can Codex handle?
items.answer: Codex can audit campaigns, surface wasted spend, find missing negatives, propose bid adjustments, and draft new ad copy. It pulls live data from your account through NotFair’s MCP tools, so answers are grounded in your real numbers — not generic best practices. Anything that touches your account is shown to you for approval before it runs.

items.question: Who is OpenAI Codex for Google Ads built for?
items.answer: Developers and technical marketers who already live in the terminal. If you run Codex CLI for code, adding NotFair lets you treat your Google Ads account the same way — describe what you want, see live data, approve changes. No CSV exports, no dashboard hopping.

items.question: Is this free?
items.answer: Yes. Adding NotFair to Codex and running a Google Ads audit is free with no credit card. Paid plans unlock higher usage limits and team features.

items.question: Can Codex actually change my Google Ads account?
items.answer: Only with your explicit approval. Codex can propose pausing campaigns, adjusting bids, adding negative keywords, or writing new ads — but every write action is shown to you first and requires your confirmation. Read access is unrestricted; write access is gated.

auditPasses.title: A prioritized fix list, not just a score.

items.name: Stop Wasting
items.description: Identifies non-converting keywords, irrelevant search terms, and structural waste — with exact dollar amounts so you know what to cut first.

items.name: Capture More
items.description: Finds budget-constrained campaigns that are winning auctions but running out of gas, plus converting search terms you haven't added as keywords yet.

items.name: Fix Fundamentals
items.description: Surfaces structural issues — conversion tracking gaps, campaign organization, ad copy quality, and bidding strategy fit — that compound over time.

items.name: Waste Rate
items.description: What percentage of your spend goes to keywords and search terms with zero conversions. Lower is better.

items.name: Demand Captured
items.description: How much of the available search demand you're actually showing up for on profitable campaigns. Higher is better.

items.name: CPA
items.description: Your cost per conversion. The audit tracks this over time so you can see the impact of changes.

findings.title: What a typical audit finds.
findings.body: Most accounts running Google Ads for 6+ months have accumulated significant waste. Here's what we commonly uncover in the first analysis.

howItWorks.title: From connect to insights in 5 minutes.

items.title: Connect Google Ads

verticals.title: Google Ads audits tuned for your vertical.

McpSetupTabs.title: Where do you want to use NotFair?
McpSetupTabs.body: Pick one setup path below. The instructions underneath update for that client.

ConnectorSetupSteps.defaultPrompt: Audit my connected {platform} account and prioritize the 3 changes most likely to improve performance. For every recommendation, you must pull actual data to prove or disprove your hypothesis before suggesting it. Then, show the hard evidence, expected upside, and the exact change you recommend I approve, and create a live dashboard for me for ongoing monitoring

step1.title: Open Claude Connectors
step1.body: Open Claude's connector settings on the web - the button below lands directly on Add custom connector. Connectors set up here apply to both Claude Desktop and Claude on the web.
step1.imageAlt: Click the plus icon in Connectors and choose Add custom connector

step2.title: Configure the connector
step2.configureAlt: Add custom connector dialog with Name and Remote MCP Server URL filled in
step2.verify: Verify {connectorName} appears in your Connectors list with all available tools.
step2.savedAlt: NotFair connector saved and listed under Connectors with its tool permissions
step2.refreshAlt: Connector menu with the Refresh tools list option highlighted

step3.title: Ask Claude about your ads
step3.beforePrompt: Open a new Claude chat and try a prompt like
step3.afterPrompt: Claude will use the NotFair connector to read your account and respond with specific, data-backed insights.
step3.imageAlt: Claude using the NotFair connector to audit a {platform} account in a chat

open.desktop: Prefer the desktop app? Open Claude Desktop and go to Customize -> Connectors -> Add custom connector.

ClaudeCodePluginSteps.defaultPrompt: Audit my connected {platform} account and prioritize the 3 changes most likely to improve performance. For every recommendation, you must pull actual data to prove or disprove your hypothesis before suggesting it. Then, show the hard evidence, expected upside, and the exact change you recommend I approve, and create a live dashboard for me for ongoing monitoring

step1.title: Open Claude Code
step1.afterLink: . If you do not have it installed, follow Anthropic's install guide first.

step2.title: Install the toprank plugin
step2.beforeLink: In Claude Code, run these slash commands to register the
step2.afterLink: marketplace, install the NotFair plugin, and reload plugins. Toprank ships with pre-made paid-ads and SEO skills that teach Claude how to audit and optimize your campaigns.

step3.title: Run {slashCommand}
step3.afterCommand: in Claude Code. It will open your browser to sign in and connect NotFair. If the command does not appear, restart Claude Code first.
step3.important: Important: sign in with the same Google account you use on NotFair. Otherwise Claude Code will connect to an empty account and will not see your {platform} data.

step4.title: Ask Claude about your ads
step4.afterPrompt: Claude will call NotFair tools to read your account and respond with specific, data-backed insights.

CodexSetupSteps.defaultPrompt: Audit my connected Google Ads account and prioritize the 3 changes most likely to improve performance. For every recommendation, you must pull actual data to prove or disprove your hypothesis before suggesting it. Then, show the hard evidence, expected upside, and the exact change you recommend I approve, and create a live dashboard for me for ongoing monitoring

step1.title: Install the Codex CLI
step1.afterLink: if you do not have it already. Follow the official install guide for your platform.

step2.title: Add the NotFair MCP
step2.body: Run this one-liner. Codex will walk you through the OAuth flow and register the NotFair MCP server automatically.

step3.title: Ask Codex about your ads
step3.afterPrompt: Codex will call NotFair tools to read your account and respond with specific, data-backed insights.

oauth.title: OAuth 2.0 (recommended)
oauth.subtitle: Drop into ~/.cursor/mcp.json (Cursor), the Cline settings JSON, or any client that takes the standard MCP config schema. The client opens a browser for sign-in.

ConnectMetaAdsMcpPage.title: Connect Meta Ads MCP
ConnectMetaAdsMcpPage.bodyBeforeCode: Wire your Claude / Codex / MCP client up to NotFair's Meta Ads resource at
ConnectMetaAdsMcpPage.bodyAfterCode: . Same setup steps as Google - just a different URL and connector name.
ConnectMetaAdsMcpPage.examplePrompt: Audit my connected Meta ad account (Facebook + Instagram) and tell me the 3 biggest optimization opportunities.

MissingPlatformWarning.title: No {platform} account linked yet.
MissingPlatformWarning.body: You can read the setup steps below, but connecting an MCP client now will not do anything until at least one {platform} account is linked.

google.title: Google Ads accounts connected.
google.body: Set up the Google Ads MCP so Claude, Codex, and other clients can use it.

meta.title: Meta ads accounts connected.
meta.body: Set up the Meta Ads MCP so Claude, Codex, and other clients can use it.

GoHighLevelConnect.title: Connect GoHighLevel
GoHighLevelConnect.body: Link agency or sub-account access so NotFair can read CRM context: contacts, conversations, opportunities, and calendar events.

access.title: Access requested
access.body: Read-only for the MVP. No contact edits, messages, or workflow changes.

model.title: Connection model
items: Supports agencies with multiple HighLevel locations.
Stores each connection by company/location identity.

signedOut.title: Sign into NotFair first
signedOut.body: Use the main connect flow first so the GoHighLevel connection has a NotFair user to attach to.

GoHighLevelConnect.marketplaceNote: You will be sent to the GoHighLevel Marketplace install flow.

AddMetaAdsAccount.title: Connect Meta Ads
AddMetaAdsAccount.bodyBeforeCode: Authorize NotFair to read and manage your Meta (Facebook + Instagram) ad accounts. Once connected, the Meta MCP at
AddMetaAdsAccount.bodyAfterCode: can be used by Claude.ai, Codex, and any other MCP client tied to your NotFair account. Switch which account you are working on from the navbar dropdown.

mcp.title: Connect to Meta Ads MCP
mcp.body: Wire Claude, Codex, or any MCP client to your Meta ad accounts.

notConnected.title: No Meta connection yet
notConnected.body: Click below to grant NotFair access to your Meta Business Manager and ad accounts. You will be redirected to Facebook to choose which assets to share, then back here to pick which ad accounts NotFair can manage.

connected.title: Choose which ad accounts NotFair can access
connected.body: Check the accounts you want NotFair to manage. Re-authorize Meta if your account list has changed or if you have added new permissions to the app.
connected.reauthorizeTitle: Run the Meta OAuth flow again to pick up new scopes or refresh the account list.
connected.emptyBeforeLink: We did not find any Meta ad accounts you can manage. Make sure the Meta account you signed in with has access to a Business Manager or a direct ad account, then

disconnect.title: Disconnect Meta Ads
disconnect.body: Removes the saved authorization, revokes the access token at Meta, and invalidates any MCP tokens issued for this connection. Your historical NotFair data is kept; you can reconnect any time.

card.body: Pending Meta App Review. Join the waitlist and we will email when it is live.

wall.title: Meta Ads is on the way
wall.body: We are finishing up Meta App Review. Join the waitlist and we will send you an email the moment NotFair can connect to your Facebook + Instagram ad accounts.
wall.note: In the meantime, NotFair fully supports Google Ads.

ConnectAdsPrompt.title: Connect your Google Ads account
ConnectAdsPrompt.body: Link your Google Ads account to start managing your campaigns with AI. You will authorize read and write access to your ad data.
